mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains at the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 8.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth concerns propagate through equity valuations.
regime_hint: labor market weakness and recessionary pressure
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 8.5%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is positive (r > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term corporate revenue growth. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when retail sales fall below the prior month's level.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of months when RSAFS declines, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS momentum and forward returns is positive (rho > 0.08) over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) below 97.5 combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 8.5%) signals simultaneous supply-side weakness and demand destruction, creating a stagflationary signal that produces negative equity returns. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both conditions to activate.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.0723 and UNRATE at 8.9% both signal weakness
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with supply and labor constraints
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods when both INDPRO < 97.5 and UNRATE > 8.5%, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 500 thousand units annualized signals residential construction weakness and reduced forward demand for materials and labor. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 510.0 thousand units near threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when HOUST < 500, or the predictive power decays to Rank IC < 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 50 thousand jobs per month combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 212) signals stagflationary conditions where the Fed must tighten despite labor weakness, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days as rate hike expectations rise.
regime_hint: stagflationary policy dilemma with conflicting labor and inflation signals
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods meeting both conditions, or the interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in a predictive model.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 213 combined with federal funds rate remaining below 0.50% signals monetary policy lag where real rates are deeply negative. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors seek inflation hedges in equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative at current snapshot
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative policy lag
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods when both conditions hold, or the real rate proxy (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL inflation rate) shows no correlation with forward returns (r < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) stagnating or declining quarter-over-quarter signals economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings and equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when GDP growth turns negative or falls below 0.5% annualized.
regime_hint: economic contraction or near-zero growth regime
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of quarters when GDP growth is negative or below 0.5%, or Rank IC between GDP growth and forward returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
